Western Federation of Miners, OF
which the accused is a member,
and endeavored to pursuade him to
testify against the officers of that
organization. At Wallace Suther-
land refused defendant privilege OF
consulting with counsel, but ar
ranged a secret meeting with De-
tective MCFARLAND, then in the en-
plov of the prosecution. Adams al
leges he was an unwilling party to
this meeting, fixed for the purpose
of entrapping him into damaging
admissions to be used as testimony.


As a sheriff is ineligible for re-
election in Idaho, it is charged that
Bailey was elected on the express
understanding that Sutherland, as
his deputy, should have full charge
of the Adams case in the interests
of the prosecution. If Sutherland
is allowed to handle the venire, it
is urged, only enemies of the West
ern Federation of Miners will be Sc
cured for the jury.


The statement of Attorney
Knight, for the prosecution, that
the federation is expanding large
sums of money to defeat the ends
of justice in defending Adams fail
ed of proof when John Hendrick-
son, former president of the local
miners union, was placed on the
stand. Hendrickson denied that
any extra assessment had been
made to meet the expense of de-
Tending the case.


Clarence barrow, of Chicago, has
arrived to take charge of the de-
Tense.
